headline: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: US envoy hopeful on Iran talks as strikes target nuclear facilities
G7 foreign ministers demand 'immediate' end to attacks against civilians in Middle East war
ICC member states divided over prosecutor Karim Khan's fate
description: A report submitted by three judges concluded there was 'insufficient evidence' to establish misconduct by Karim Khan. However, member states remain divided on what steps to take next.

headline: War in Iran: Israel and Gulf states fear a hasty US exit
description: Under pressure from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the US president could decide to call a ceasefire, but that would resolve neither the issue of access to this maritime passage nor the question of the Iranian threat in the region.

headline: EU is targeting small parcels from Shein, Temu and other Chinese e-commerce sites
description: Non-European platforms will now be legally responsible for products entering the European Union and will be taxed starting in July.

headline: Israeli writer Etgar Keret wonders if life 'must be reduced to passively reacting to the orders of warlords'
description: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 4/4. Le Monde asked Sinan Antoon (Iraq), Etgar Keret (Israel) and Diane Mazloum (France, Lebanon) for their perspectives on the war.
byline: Etgar Keret,  Diane Mazloum and  Sinan Antoon

In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
description: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
description: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.
